description: Pi.dev enhancement task list. Ask Pi to work through these one by one. Each task is self-contained.
instructions: Say to Pi: 'Read docs/pi-tasks.json and complete task N. When done, mark it completed and tell me what you did.'

tasks.title: Install oh-my-pi wrapper
tasks.description: Install the oh-my-pi community wrapper which provides pre-built extension bundles, alternative runtimes (Bun), and pre-configured language server integrations.
steps: Check if oh-my-pi is available at https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono or npm
Install via npm or their install script
Verify it doesn't conflict with existing ~/.pi/agent/ setup
Document what it added
tasks.notes: May provide automated install of many other extensions. Do this before installing individual extensions.

tasks.title: Install pure-focus extension
tasks.description: Strips away the native footer bar and status line entirely for a distraction-free terminal environment.
steps: Find or create the pure-focus.ts extension
Save to ~/.pi/agent/extensions/pure-focus.ts
Test by launching: pi --extension ~/.pi/agent/extensions/pure-focus.ts
Add a launch alias or justfile recipe

tasks.title: Install minimal extension
tasks.description: Replaces the standard UI with a compact footer showing only model name and a 10-block context usage meter.
steps: Find or create the minimal.ts extension
Save to ~/.pi/agent/extensions/minimal.ts
Test by launching: pi --extension ~/.pi/agent/extensions/minimal.ts

tasks.title: Install tool-counter extension
tasks.description: Adds a two-line telemetry footer: model + context meter + token/cost stats on line 1, CWD + git branch + tool call counts on line 2.
steps: Find or create the tool-counter.ts extension
Save to ~/.pi/agent/extensions/tool-counter.ts
Test by launching: pi --extension ~/.pi/agent/extensions/tool-counter.ts

tasks.title: Install purpose-gate extension
tasks.description: Forces explicit session intent declaration before Pi responds. Renders a persistent purpose widget and blocks all LLM prompts until intent is set.
steps: Find or create the purpose-gate.ts extension
Save to ~/.pi/agent/extensions/purpose-gate.ts
Test by launching: pi --extension ~/.pi/agent/extensions/purpose-gate.ts

tasks.title: Install cross-agent extension
tasks.description: Recursively scans for .claude/, .gemini/, .codex/ directories and imports their commands, skills, and instructions into Pi. Useful for projects that also have Claude Code or Gemini CLI configs.
steps: Find or create the cross-agent.ts extension
Save to ~/.pi/agent/extensions/cross-agent.ts
Test in a project directory that has a CLAUDE.md or .claude/ dir
Verify Claude Code skills/commands are available in Pi

tasks.title: Install till-done extension
tasks.description: Injects a strict state-machine into the agent loop that prevents Pi from declaring a task complete until it passes verification. Transforms Pi from a conversational assistant into a relentless execution engine for long autonomous tasks.
steps: Find or create the till-done.ts extension
Understand the state machine: task declared → implement → verify → pass or retry
Save to ~/.pi/agent/extensions/till-done.ts
Test on a small multi-step coding task

tasks.title: Set up Justfile orchestration
tasks.description: Create a justfile at the project root that orchestrates Pi launches with specific extension combinations. Handles dotenv loading and spawns isolated terminal windows per session type.
steps: Install just: https://github.com/casey/just — on WSL2: cargo install just OR apt install just
Create /mnt/c/Users/HAAK/pi.dev/justfile
Add recipes: open <extension>, open-team <ext1> <ext2>, open-ralph, open-focused
Use set dotenv-load so .env credentials are injected before Pi starts
Test: just open pure-focus
tasks.example:
just open tool-counter
just open-team pure-focus tool-counter
just open-ralph

tasks.title: Set up pi-teams multi-agent extension
tasks.description: Installs the pi-teams extension which enables Claude Code-style autonomous sub-agents. Define teams in teams.yaml with planner, security, reviewer roles each using different models.
tasks.source: Search npm for pi-teams or https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono
steps: Find pi-teams package or extension
Create teams.yaml with at least one team definition
Test with a simple 2-agent team (planner + reviewer)
Document the team configuration in this project

tasks.title: Set up Pi Ralph loop
tasks.description: Create the file infrastructure for running autonomous Ralph-style loops inside Pi. Enables long-running autonomous coding sessions with compaction notes, progress tracking, and fail logs.
steps: Create .pi/ralph/ directory
Create .pi/ralph/progress.md template
Create .pi/ralph/fail_log.md template
Add Ralph Loop Protocol section to AGENTS.md (already partially done)
Create a run-ralph.sh launcher script that calls pi with --max-turns 80
Test on a small PLAN.md with 3 tasks
tasks.loop_command: pi --provider openrouter --model anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6 --thinking medium --max-turns 80 -p 'Run the Ralph loop in PLAN.md' 2>&1 | tee .pi/ralph/session-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log

tasks.title: Add OS-level sandboxing for WSL2
tasks.description: Add bubblewrap-based sandbox to confine Pi's file system and network access. Prevents runaway bash commands from touching ~/.ssh, .env files, or making unauthorized outbound requests.
steps: Install bubblewrap in WSL2: sudo apt-get install bubblewrap
Check if @anthropic-ai/sandbox-runtime is available for Pi
Create .pi/sandbox.json with filesystem restrictions and network allowlist
Test that Pi can still write to project dir but not to ~/.ssh/
Document the sandbox config

tasks.title: Add agent-chain extension for pipeline workflows
tasks.description: Install agent-chain which pipes the output of one Pi agent directly into the input of the next. Enables automated data refinement pipelines (e.g. research → summarize → format → publish).
tasks.source: Search npm or https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono for agent-chain
steps: Find agent-chain package
Define a simple 2-step pipeline (e.g. fetch + summarize)
Test the pipeline
Add a justfile recipe for common chains

tasks.title: Configure mid-session model switching favorites
tasks.description: Set up a curated 'scoped' model rotation list so Ctrl+P cycles through a short list of favorites rather than all models. Optimizes the fast-switch workflow: Haiku for exploration, Sonnet for implementation, Opus for architecture.
steps: Find where Pi's model rotation list is configured (likely settings.json or models.json)
Define a scoped rotation: [haiku, sonnet, opus]
Test Ctrl+P cycles correctly
Document the shortcut workflow

tasks.title: Create project-specific AGENTS.md for pi.dev itself
tasks.description: Add a root AGENTS.md to the pi.dev project directory so Pi knows the project structure, how to run tests, and what rules apply when working on pi.dev itself.
steps: Create /mnt/c/Users/HAAK/pi.dev/AGENTS.md
Include: project overview, test commands (bash tests/test-guard.sh, bash tests/test-runner.sh), install scripts location, Docker image name, key files
Commit to git

tasks.title: Upgrade playwright-safe guard to LLM critic (phase 2)
tasks.description: Implement the LLM critic hook that was stubbed in guard.sh. Uses a cheap model (Haiku) to evaluate whether page content contains agent-directed instructions vs normal content, as a second layer after regex.
steps: Read the LLM CRITIC HOOK comment in skills/playwright-safe/guard/guard.sh
Decide on the critic model (haiku via OpenRouter is cheapest)
Implement the critic call: pipe suspicious content to the model with a YES/NO prompt
Add critic risk_score escalation logic
Update tests/test-guard.sh with critic-specific test cases
Update the spec in docs/superpowers/specs/

tasks.title: Add more test coverage for playwright-safe
tasks.description: The current tests cover goto and guard basics. Add tests for all 6 operations, boundary conditions, and malformed input.
missing_tests: guard: string at exactly 200-char boundary (validates scoring branch)
runner: screenshot (verify file created in screenshots/)
cli: end-to-end with injection payload (full pipeline test)
